_comment_data_level: Input product: 'L1' (rcs_0 daily), 'L2_daily' or 'L2_monthly' (attenuated_backscatter_0 reconstructed). folder_root must match.
_comment_abs_cs: Empty = use the bundled 910 nm WV LUT (calibration/data/abs_cross_wv_910nm.nc). Set a path to override with a full-band external LUT.
_comment_night: Night selection for the (night-only) Rayleigh fit. use_sza_night=1 -> darkness-adaptive window: keep profiles with solar zenith angle > sza_night_threshold deg (100 = sun ~10 deg below horizon; 108 = astronomical, strictest). use_sza_night=0 -> fixed solar-clock window hour_min..hour_max. SZA falls back to the clock window if station coordinates are missing.
_comment_time_aggregation: Time collapse before the molecular fit: 'mean' (efficient for the weak-signal photon noise at 3-6 km) with 'screen_profile_outliers' to drop residual-aerosol/cloud/noise profiles first. A plain 'median' profile is robust to outliers but ~1.5x noisier at weak-signal altitudes (worse night-to-night), so screen+mean is preferred.
_comment_cloud: Full profiles are excluded if the cloud base is below this altitude
_comment_range: Molecular window search: center position between these values
_comment_window_gates: Molecular-window validity gates (reject aerosol/low-SNR; see rayleigh_fit.find_optimal_molecular_window). min_window_start_m: the window must START above this altitude (m AGL), i.e. above the boundary-layer aerosol (rec #1). min_window_r2: minimum R^2 of the signal-vs-molecular linear fit (cf. MATLAB min_r2_rfit=0.5). max_window_rel_error: max % deviation of the fit slope from the pointwise median ratio (rejects aerosol curvature, rec #2). Best window = highest R^2 among eligible windows; if none qualifies the night is flagged non-calibration instead of emitting a spurious low-R^2 constant.
_comment_molecular_method: Molecular-window detection strategy (molecular_methods.py): 'improved' (default, production) | 'main' (legacy degenerate, for comparison) | 'matlab' (Auto_Calib_25: forced b=0, min ΣRMSE, R² floor) | 'calipso' (highest clean layer, scattering ratio→1) | 'earlinet' (lowest qualifying, shape+SNR) | 'optimal' (composite). The min_window_*/max_window_* gates apply to 'improved'; other methods use their own defaults.
_comment_molecular: Molecular reference source (beta_mol ~ P/T): 'standard' (US Std 1976, matches MATLAB) or 'cams' (actual CAMS T/p, same archive as the WV correction). <1% difference at mid-latitudes.
_comment_wv: Water-vapor correction for 910 nm instruments (CL31/CL51/CL61). A 910 nm night without matching CAMS is skipped. 1064 nm (CHM15k) is unaffected.
